issue.author: clin111
issue.body:
After https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/commit/0fc63d4e642fcf79e9806dc3ae7a49defaae2479, the Attributor may remove stores from the IR without propagating the values.
Attached test case:
```shell
opt -S -passes=attributor reduced.ll
```
The attached `.pdf` file has a graph of the IR.
The IR contains the following stores:
bb: 0.0 => `%alloca3`
bb5: `%arg2` => `%alloca3`
bb9: 1.0 => `%alloca`
bb18: (loaded value of `%alloca3`) => `%alloca`

The final value of `%alloca` could be 0.0, 1.0, `%arg2`, or undef.
After the Attributor runs, `%alloca3` and the associated loads and stores have been removed, but the values 0.0 and `%arg2` have not been propagated anywhere. The only value stored to `%alloca` is 1.0.
Commenting this code in AttributorAttributes seems to fix it, which is strange because "false" should be the conservative answer.
```cpp
if(CyclePHI && isa<Instruction>(V) &&
            (!C || C->contains(cast<Instruction>(V)->getParent())))
          return false;
```

[reduced.ll.txt](https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/files/11128574/reduced.ll.txt)
[reduced.pdf](https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/files/11128575/reduced.pdf)

comments.author: jdoerfert
comments.body:
The commit you pointed at just exposed a bug I introduced earlier.
Apologies for the delay.

Should be fixed now.

issue.title: [Attributor] Miscompile after phi fix

tests.test_body:
define ptr @checkAndAdvance(ptr align 16 %0) {
  %2 = load i32, ptr %0, align 4
  %3 = icmp eq i32 %2, 0
  br i1 %3, label %4, label %7

4:                                                ; preds = %1
  %5 = getelementptr inbounds i32, ptr %0, i64 4
  %6 = call ptr @checkAndAdvance(ptr %5)
  br label %8

7:                                                ; preds = %1
  br label %8

8:                                                ; preds = %7, %4
  %.0 = phi ptr [ %6, %4 ], [ %0, %7 ]
  ret ptr %.0
}

@random = external global i1, align 4

define void @widget(ptr %arg1, float %arg2, i64 %idx1, i64 %idx2, i32 %limit) {
bb:
  %alloca = alloca [128 x float], i32 0, align 4
  %alloca3 = alloca [81 x float], i32 0, align 4
  store float 0.000000e+00, ptr %alloca3, align 4
  br label %bb4

bb4:                                              ; preds = %bb5, %bb
  %c1 = load volatile i1, ptr @random, align 1
  br i1 %c1, label %bb5, label %bb7

bb5:                                              ; preds = %bb4
  store float %arg2, ptr %alloca3, align 4
  br label %bb4

bb7:                                              ; preds = %bb11, %bb4
  %phi = phi i32 [ %add, %bb11 ], [ 0, %bb4 ]
  %icmp = icmp slt i32 %phi, 5
  br i1 %icmp, label %bb8, label %bb12

bb8:                                              ; preds = %bb9, %bb7
  %c2 = load volatile i1, ptr @random, align 1
  br i1 %c2, label %bb9, label %bb11

bb9:                                              ; preds = %bb8
  %sext = sext i32 %phi to i64
  %getelementptr10 = getelementptr [128 x float], ptr %alloca, i64 0, i64 %sext
  store float 1.000000e+00, ptr %getelementptr10, align 4
  br label %bb8

bb11:                                             ; preds = %bb8
  %add = add i32 1, %phi
  br label %bb7

bb12:                                             ; preds = %bb7
  %c3 = load volatile i1, ptr @random, align 1
  br i1 %c3, label %bb13, label %bb19

bb13:                                             ; preds = %bb12
  %load = load float, ptr %alloca3, align 4
  br label %bb14

bb14:                                             ; preds = %bb17, %bb13
  %phi15 = phi i32 [ 0, %bb13 ], [ 1, %bb17 ]
  %icmp16 = icmp slt i32 %phi15, 1
  br i1 %icmp16, label %bb17, label %bb19

bb17:                                             ; preds = %bb18, %bb14
  %c4 = load volatile i1, ptr @random, align 1
  br i1 %c4, label %bb18, label %bb14

bb18:                                             ; preds = %bb17
  store float %load, ptr %alloca, align 4
  br label %bb17

bb19:                                             ; preds = %bb14, %bb12
  %sext20 = sext i32 0 to i64
  %load22 = load float, ptr %alloca, align 4
  store float %load22, ptr %arg1, align 4
  ret void
}
